chapter_context.summary: Jonathan and David affirm their eternal covenant of friendship; Jonathan uses three arrows to warn David of Saul's deadly intentions.

outline.text: David and Jonathan Consult; David's Fear of Saul
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David and Jonathan Consult; David's Fear of Saul (20:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Jonathan and David Renew Their Covenant at the Field
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jonathan and David Renew Their Covenant at the Field (20:11-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Saul's Fury at David's Absence; Saul Spears Jonathan
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Saul's Fury at David's Absence; Saul Spears Jonathan (20:24-34), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Jonathan Warns David with Arrows; Emotional Farewell
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jonathan Warns David with Arrows; Emotional Farewell (20:35-42), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 20, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
